preamble: The shift from the near-open front unrounded vowel [æ] (ash) to the open front unrounded vowel [a] is a form of vowel lowering. This change simplifies the vowel space by moving the tongue to a maximally open position.

examples.note: Part of the chain shift where /æ/ lowers and backs in some American dialects.
General lowering of short front vowels in North Germanic developments.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change.
